---
title: "Integrals"
author: "Aleksandra"
date: "2026-02-27"
categories: [math, interactive]
image: "thumb.jpeg"
description: ""
title-block-banner: true
include-in-header: libs.html
---

## Constant Function

Let's look at the simplest case: $f(t) = 5$. The integral $\int_a^b 5 \, d\tau = 5(b - a)$.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

source("plot_helpers.R")

render_interactive_plot(
  js_file = "plot_constant_integral.js",
  init_function = "initConstantIntegralPlot",
  controls_html = create_integral_controls(
    id_prefix = "Constant",
    range_min = 0, range_max = 10,
    default_lower = 0, default_upper = 5,
    update_function = "updateConstantIntegralPlot",
    bg_color = "rgba(147, 38, 103, 0.04)"
  ),
  plot_containers = create_plot_containers(id_prefix = "Constant")
)
```

## Interactive Integral Visualization

Use the slider below to change the upper limit of integration. The left plot shows the function $f(t) = t$ with the area under the curve shaded. The dashed outline shows the maximum possible area. The right plot shows the value of the integral $\int_0^t \tau \, d\tau = \frac{t^2}{2}$.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

render_interactive_plot(
  js_file = "plot_integral.js",
  init_function = "initIntegralPlot",
  controls_html = create_integral_controls(
    id_prefix = "",
    range_min = 0, range_max = 10,
    default_lower = 0, default_upper = 5,
    update_function = "updateIntegralPlot",
    bg_color = "rgba(147, 38, 103, 0.04)"
  ),
  plot_containers = create_plot_containers(id_prefix = "")
)
```

## Logarithmic Function

Now let's look at $f(t) = \ln(t)$. The integral $\int_1^t \ln(\tau) \, d\tau = t \cdot \ln(t) - t + 1$.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

render_interactive_plot(
  js_file = "plot_log_integral.js",
  init_function = "initLogIntegralPlot",
  controls_html = create_integral_controls(
    id_prefix = "Log",
    range_min = 1, range_max = 20,
    default_lower = 1, default_upper = 10,
    update_function = "updateLogIntegralPlot",
    bg_color = "rgba(147, 38, 103, 0.04)"
  ),
  plot_containers = create_plot_containers(id_prefix = "Log")
)
```

## Exponential Function

Now let's look at $f(t) = e^t$. The integral $\int_0^t e^\tau \, d\tau = e^t - 1$.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

render_interactive_plot(
  js_file = "plot_exp_integral.js",
  init_function = "initExpIntegralPlot",
  controls_html = create_integral_controls(
    id_prefix = "Exp",
    range_min = 0, range_max = 5,
    default_lower = 0, default_upper = 2.5,
    update_function = "updateExpIntegralPlot",
    bg_color = "rgba(147, 38, 103, 0.04)"
  ),
  plot_containers = create_plot_containers(id_prefix = "Exp")
)
```

## Sigmoid Function

The sigmoid function $\sigma(t) = \frac{1}{1+e^{-t}}$ is widely used in machine learning. Its integral is $\int_a^b \sigma(\tau) \, d\tau = \ln(1+e^b) - \ln(1+e^a)$.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

render_interactive_plot(
  js_file = "plot_sigmoid_integral.js",
  init_function = "initSigmoidIntegralPlot",
  controls_html = create_integral_controls(
    id_prefix = "Sigmoid",
    range_min = -5, range_max = 5,
    default_lower = -2, default_upper = 2,
    update_function = "updateSigmoidIntegralPlot",
    bg_color = "rgba(147, 38, 103, 0.04)"
  ),
  plot_containers = create_plot_containers(id_prefix = "Sigmoid")
)
```
